bestStrategy.label: No IP · No new invest · Debt down · Risk low · FIRE 2050
bestStrategy.whyRobust: No IP · No new invest · Debt down · Risk low · FIRE 2050 delivers 100% probability of reaching FIRE on schedule, 50% Monte Carlo confidence, 0% liquidity-stress risk.
bestStrategy.whatBreaks: What could break this plan: 100% chance of negative cashflow.

audit.label: Assumption Set
audit.howCalculated: Each driver is drawn independently every simulation step as a clamped Normal(μ, σ). Multipliers compose into per-trial perturbations of the Sprint 7 engine outputs — no new financial formula is introduced.
assumptions: Property capital growth: μ=1, σ=0.04, [0.85, 1.15]
Interest rates: μ=1, σ=0.03, [0.9, 1.15]
ETF / stock return: μ=1, σ=0.05, [0.8, 1.2]
Crypto return: μ=1, σ=0.15, [0.6, 1.4] — not engine-modelled
Income growth: μ=1, σ=0.02, [0.93, 1.1]
Expense inflation: μ=1, σ=0.02, [0.95, 1.08]
Maintenance cost: μ=1, σ=0.03, [0.9, 1.2] — not engine-modelled
Selling cost: μ=1, σ=0.02, [0.95, 1.1] — not engine-modelled
Debt-service stress: μ=1, σ=0.03, [0.92, 1.15]

audit.label: Simulation Metadata
audit.howCalculated: Per-strategy independent PRNG ⇒ reproducible totals. Bands are rounded to nearest $1,000 (net worth/passive income), nearest year (FIRE year), and nearest dollar (required contribution). Probabilities round to integer percent.
assumptions: Deterministic mulberry32 PRNG, seed offset 7919 per strategy.
Standard Normal via Box–Muller.
P10/P50/P90 are sample percentiles over the trial samples.

audit.label: Probability Derivations
audit.howCalculated: Each probability is the share of trials satisfying the predicate. Counts use only the trials where the underlying engine baseline existed — missing baselines flow to incomplete rather than fake 0%/100% values.
assumptions: Liquidity stress threshold = 3 months (matches canonicalRiskSurface).
Negative cashflow proxy = (passive income / 12) − required monthly contribution.
Forced sale proxy = projected net worth < 0 (Sprint 7 net worth already nets liabilities).
FIRE success = simulated FIRE year ≤ target FIRE year (when supplied) else ≤ goal-reverse-engineering target year.

audit.label: Robust Strategy Ranking
audit.howCalculated: Strategies sorted by robustScore desc. Ties broken by simulation order which is itself seeded ⇒ reproducible.
assumptions: Robust score = 0.5 × deterministic ranking + 0.5 × Monte Carlo confidence.
Monte Carlo confidence = P(FIRE) − 0.5 × P(liquidity stress) − 0.5 × P(negative cashflow) − 0.5 × P(forced sale).
When either component is missing, robust score falls back to whichever is available — never invented.

audit.label: Assumption Sensitivity
inputsUsed: AssumptionSet, AssumptionSet (with each σ × 2 in turn)
audit.howCalculated: Per-driver delta vs the baseline assumption set is the simplest single-factor sensitivity — it reveals which assumption the strategy is most fragile to without introducing a new model.
assumptions: Each driver is stress-tested by doubling its σ and re-running 500 trials against the leading strategy.
Sensitivity column 'Δ P(success)' is integer percentage points; column 'Δ P50 net worth' is rounded to nearest $1,000.

audit.label: Sprint 7 Pass-Through Provenance
audit.howCalculated: Sprint 8 strategies inherit Sprint 7 ranking, frontier, and audit. Every probability surfaced here cites its Sprint 7 baseline.
assumptions: Sprint 8 reads only Sprint 7 outputs and canonical engine values quoted in Sprint 7 audit. No engine bypassed.
Sprint 7 search metrics: generated=3168, valid=3168, frontier=1602.
